question_title: Will El Paso, Texas receive any rain in the 2nd half of April 2026?
summary: El Paso is a desert city where April is one of the driest months (avg 0.17-0.23 inches). The question asks whether at least 0.01 inches of precipitation will be recorded at ELP airport on any day from April 15-30 (16-day window). A Poisson model calibrated to the recent decade gives ~39% base rate for the 2nd half of April; the long-term historical record gives ~58%. Blending these and adjusting for: (1) NWS forecast showing dry conditions through April 18, (2) a heavily discounted extended forecast signal for thunderstorms around April 25, and (3) the very low 0.01" threshold, produces a central estimate of ~45%. Sensitivity analysis shows the estimate is stable at 40-52% across reasonable parameter variations. The question is genuinely close to a coin flip, with the status quo (dry) slightly favored.

factors.description: El Paso is a desert city with April as one of its driest months (avg 0.17-0.23 inches total). Recent decade shows 40% of Aprils with no measurable precipitation at all.
16-day window (April 15-30) with very low threshold (0.01 inches counts as YES). This provides many opportunities for even brief showers to register.
NWS 7-day forecast shows dry conditions through April 18, effectively removing 4 of 16 days from the window.
Extended 14-day forecast (timeanddate.com) shows 63% chance of thunderstorms on April 25 with 10.2mm expected. But at 13 days out, forecast skill for desert precipitation is very low.
Old Farmer's Almanac says 'sunny west' for El Paso area for most of late April, with thunderstorms concentrated in eastern Texas.
Climatological base rate from Poisson model: ~39% (recent decade) to ~58% (long-term) probability of at least one measurable rain day in 2nd half of April. Blended estimate ~46%.
IEM data shows April 2026 already had 0.01" on April 6 and April 11, demonstrating the atmosphere can currently produce measurable precipitation, though barely above threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.45 (45% probability of YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Base rate**: El Paso's April is extremely dry. Recent decade shows 40% of Aprils with zero measurable precipitation. Poisson modeling gives a 39-58% base rate for at least one measurable rain day in the 2nd half of April, depending on historical window used.
- **Near-term forecast**: NWS shows dry through April 18 with high confidence, removing 4 of 16 days from the window.
- **Extended outlook**: One extended forecast hints at thunderstorms around April 25, but at 13 days out this has very low reliability. The Almanac suggests El Paso specifically stays dry while storms affect eastern Texas.
- **Low threshold helps YES**: Only 0.01 inches is needed, meaning even a brief isolated shower at the airport resolves YES.
- **Sensitivity**: The estimate is stable at 40-52% across reasonable assumptions about historical rates and forecast weighting.
sources_consulted: [El Paso weather in April 2026 | El Paso 14 day weather](https://www.weather25.com/north-america/usa/texas/el-paso?page=month&month=April)
[(Metaculus) Will El Paso, Texas receive any rain in the 2nd half of April 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43132)
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as Yes if according to the data calendar for the National Weather Service's El Paso International Airport weather station, there is any material precipitation observed for any day after April 14, 2026 and before May 1, 2026: [https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/sites/hist.phtml?station=ELP\&network=TX\_ASOS](https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/sites/hist.phtml?station=ELP\&network=TX_ASOS)

For purposes of this question, days with a recording of 0.00 inches, Trace, and M (missing) will all be considered to have had no material precipitation.
